name: Sauti za Busara
description: East Africa's best-known music festival celebrating Swahili culture and African music.

name: Cape Town International Jazz Festival
description: Major international jazz event and one of Africa's best-known urban festivals.

name: Marrakech Popular Arts Festival
description: Traditional music, storytelling, dance, and performance art across Marrakech.

name: Kirstenbosch Summer Concerts
description: Outdoor concert series in Cape Town's botanical gardens with sunset energy and picnic-friendly crowds.

name: Zanzibar International Film Festival
description: Swahili and African cinema, live performances, and waterfront cultural energy.

name: Victoria Falls Carnival Week
description: An end-of-year celebration combining concerts, adventure tourism, and holiday travel momentum.

name: Timkat Festival
description: Ethiopian Orthodox Epiphany celebration featuring spectacular processions of the Tabot (Ark replica), mass baptisms, drumming, and white-robed pilgrims at Fasilides Bath.

name: Ouidah Voodoo Festival
description: Benin's most colourful spiritual festival celebrating Vodun religion with drumming, dancing, ceremonies, sacrifices, and vibrant processions drawing diaspora from Brazil and the Caribbean.

name: Durbar Festival — Eid al-Fitr
description: Royal horseback procession marking the end of Ramadan. Emirs ride at the head of cavalry, trumpeters fill the air, and riders charge in formation before palace gates in colourful robes.

name: Homowo Festival
description: Harvest festival of the Ga people meaning 'hooting at hunger.' Features sacred kpokpoi cooking, ancestral libations, drumming, singing, and joyful parades led by chiefs and priests.

name: PANAFEST — Pan African Historical Theatre Festival
description: Ghana's biggest cultural gathering celebrating Pan-African heritage. Features durbar of chiefs at Elmina Castle, atonement rituals, naming ceremonies, and memorial services at slave trade sites.

name: Gerewol Festival
description: Spectacular Wodaabe male beauty and courtship contest. Men paint faces elaborately, wear jewellery, and perform the Yaake dance to attract potential wives. Also features camel racing and traditional feasting.

name: Eyo Festival
description: Iconic Yoruba masquerade festival where thousands of Eyo masqueraders in white robes and tall hats parade through Lagos Island streets honouring Yoruba royalty and the deceased.

name: Calabar Carnival
description: Africa's Biggest Street Party. A month-long celebration featuring carnival parades (Dec 26–30), cultural displays, music, dance, beauty pageants, and food from Nigeria's diverse ethnic groups.

name: FESPACO — Pan-African Film Festival
description: Africa's largest film festival showcasing films reflecting diverse African cultures and stories. Biennial event serving as a major platform for continental filmmakers, artists, and cultural exchange.

name: FESTIMA — Festival International des Masques et des Arts
description: West Africa's largest celebration of traditional mask cultures bringing together over 50 ethnic groups from Burkina Faso, Mali, Benin, Côte d'Ivoire, Togo, and Senegal.

name: Fes Festival of World Sacred Music
description: Global musicians perform sacred and spiritual music in the historic medina venues of Fes. One of Morocco's most important cultural gatherings blending faith, heritage, and world music.

name: Gnaoua World Music Festival
description: Celebrates the spiritual healing music of the Gnaoua people alongside world music artists. The coastal city of Essaouira transforms into a vibrant hub with concerts, workshops, and street performances.

name: Lake of Stars Festival
description: International 3-day music festival on the shores of Lake Malawi blending local Malawian musicians with international bands, DJs, talks, poetry, film, and wellness activities.

name: Umhlanga Reed Dance
description: Annual royal ceremony where thousands of unmarried Swazi girls present freshly cut reeds to the Queen Mother. Features royal dance attended by the King, traditional beadwork, and national cultural pride.

name: Harare International Festival of the Arts (HIFA)
description: Zimbabwe's premier arts festival featuring theatre, dance, visual arts, circus, music, and street performance from local and international artists across multiple Harare venues.

name: FESPAD — Pan African Dance Festival
description: Rwanda's flagship dance festival featuring iconic Intore warrior dancers with flowing white manes alongside contemporary troupes from across the African Union, bridging ancient choreography with modern street dance.

name: Lamu Cultural Festival
description: Celebration of Swahili coastal culture in a UNESCO World Heritage town. Features dhow races, donkey competitions, traditional music, Kikoy and Kanga costume parades, and a boat regatta at Fort Jesus beach.

name: Nyege Nyege International Music Festival
description: One of East Africa's biggest underground music festivals held on the banks of the Nile River. Features experimental electronic music, traditional sounds, and artists from across Africa and the world.

name: Meskel Festival
description: Ethiopian Orthodox celebration marking the Finding of the True Cross. Massive communal bonfires (Demera) are lit across the country with processions, chanting, drumming, and thousands of white-robed pilgrims.

name: Saint-Louis Jazz Festival
description: One of Africa's most iconic jazz events held in the historic island city of Saint-Louis. Blends world-class jazz performers with Senegal's rich Afro-jazz heritage in open-air riverside venues.

name: Dak'Art Biennale of Contemporary African Art
description: Africa's foremost contemporary art biennale transforming Dakar into a city-wide gallery. Features installations, exhibitions, performances, and dialogue around African and global identity.

name: Omagongo Cultural Festival
description: Celebrates the marula fruit harvest among the Ovambo people through shared meals, traditional music, storytelling, communal dances, and cultural pride.

name: Abu Simbel Sun Festival
description: Ancient astronomical spectacle occurring twice yearly (February and October) when the rising sun illuminates the inner sanctuary of Ramesses II's temple. Draws thousands of pilgrims and tourists.

name: Abu Simbel Sun Festival (October Edition)
description: The October edition of the biannual solar alignment festival at Ramesses II's temple. The sun penetrates the temple depths illuminating the pharaoh's statue in a spectacular ancient engineering feat.

name: Festival of the Sahara — Douz
description: Desert cultural festival celebrating Saharan nomadic heritage with traditional music, camel displays, equestrian shows, crafts, local food, and performances by Bedouin communities.

name: Timgad International Music Festival
description: Open-air music festival held in the extraordinary ruins of the Roman city of Timgad, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Features classical, world, and Algerian folk music performances.

name: Madajazzcar Festival
description: Premier jazz celebration across multiple venues in Madagascar's capital. International musicians and thousands of visitors attend over a fortnight. The Indian Ocean region's most acclaimed jazz event.

name: Marsabit Lake Turkana Cultural Festival
description: Brings together multiple ethnic groups from across northern Kenya to promote unity via traditional music, dance, dialogue, and celebration of nomadic pastoralist cultures at the world's largest permanent desert lake.

name: Bayimba International Festival of Music and Arts
description: Uganda's vibrant platform celebrating African music and arts featuring over 400 musicians from across the region over five days including theatre, visual arts, and dance.

name: Akwasidae Royal Festival
description: Held every six weeks at Manhyia Palace, the Ashanti King presides over lavish ceremonies honouring ancestors and the sacred Golden Stool. Chiefs in colourful regalia, drummers, and dancers pay homage to Ashanti heritage.

name: Cape Town Minstrel Carnival (Kaapse Klopse)
description: Vibrant New Year street parade with tens of thousands of costumed minstrels marching through Cape Town streets with music, song, and dance. A Cape Town tradition dating back centuries.

name: Chale Wote Street Art Festival
description: Africa's most explosive urban arts festival transforming Accra's James Town streets into a massive open-air gallery with murals, avant-garde fashion, live graffiti, music performances, and pan-African creative expression.

name: AfroFuture Ghana (formerly Afrochella)
description: Ghana's premier pan-African innovation and music festival drawing 60,000+ attendees. Features Afrobeats headliners, art installations, food markets, fashion showcases, and panel discussions on African identity.

name: Durbar Festival — Eid al-Adha
description: Second of the year's grand Durbar celebrations marking Eid al-Adha. Emirs in regal regalia lead cavalry charges before palace walls accompanied by hausa praise chants and traditional trumpeters.

name: Osun-Oshogbo Sacred Grove Festival
description: UNESCO-listed Yoruba religious festival honouring the river goddess Osun. Features sacrificial offerings, spiritual rituals, processions through the sacred grove, drumming, and chanting by devotees.

name: Baía das Gatas Music Festival
location: Baía das Gatas, São Vicente, Cape Verde
description: Cape Verde's premier beach music festival featuring morna, coladeira, Cabo love, and international acts on a natural seaside stage. One of the Atlantic island nation's most beloved cultural celebrations.

name: Klein Karoo Nasionale Kunstefees (KKNK)
description: Celebrated Afrikaans arts festival with over 100 theatre, music, and visual arts productions, markets, comedy, and diverse cultural entertainment in the Klein Karoo.

name: Ultra South Africa
description: Africa's largest electronic dance music festival, part of Ultra Music Festival's global expansion. Draws 55,000+ attendees across single-day events in both Johannesburg and Cape Town.

name: Moussem of Moulay Idris II
description: Morocco's most important religious pilgrimage gathering in honour of the founder of Morocco's first Islamic state, blending communal prayer, traditional markets, Sufi music, and social exchange.

name: Kilifi New Year Festival
description: Boutique coastal festival on Kenya's coast blending psytrance, wellness, fire dancing, and electronic music. Workshops by day, deep dance floors by night with a boho, conscious community spirit.

name: Asanteman Odwira Festival
description: Annual purification and harvest festival of the Ashanti Kingdom featuring week-long ceremonies, ancestral rites, colourful durbars of chiefs, traditional drumming, and the wearing of kente cloth.

name: Gaborone International Music & Cultural Festival (GIMC)
description: Weeklong festival celebrating Gaborone City through music, theatre, comedy, poetry, fashion, jazz, and golf. Features local, regional, and international performers with proceeds supporting community NGOs.

name: Mombasa Carnival
description: Kenya's biggest yearly street carnival organized by the Ministry of Tourism. Features floats, traditional costumes, music ranging from tribal drums to modern beats, street food, and a grand boat regatta at Fort Jesus.

name: Maputo International Music Festival
description: Mozambique's leading music showcase celebrating marrabenta, afrojazz, and contemporary sounds from across southern Africa and the globe. Held across Maputo's venues and open-air stages.

name: Nairobi International Film Festival (NIFF)
description: Premier East African film platform showcasing African and international cinema across Nairobi's venues with workshops, panel discussions, and industry networking for filmmakers.

name: Cotton Fest
description: South Africa's premier youth culture festival celebrating local and upcoming South African musical talent across multiple stages, blending Amapiano, hip-hop, and Afrobeats with fashion and creative arts.

name: Mangaung African Cultural Festival (MACUFE)
description: Free State's flagship cultural festival featuring gospel, comedy, sports, traditional arts, and music performances celebrating South African cultural diversity and heritage.

name: Cairo International Film Festival
description: One of the oldest and most prestigious film festivals in the Arab world and Africa. Showcases hundreds of films from across the globe including a strong Arab and African cinema competition.

name: Festival in the Desert (Festival au Désert)
description: Legendary desert music gathering of the Tuareg people and global world music artists. Features traditional Tuareg guitar music (desert blues), camel races, and cultural exchange under the Saharan stars.

name: Abuja Carnival
description: Nigeria's national capital carnival celebrating the country's cultural diversity with colourful floats, traditional costumes, music and dance from all 36 states, and grand street parades.

name: Guinée Guinée Cultural Festival
description: Celebrates Guinea's rich jali (griot) musical heritage, djembe drumming tradition, and the vibrant balafon music culture of West Africa through concerts, dance competitions, and artisan markets.

name: MASA — Marché des Arts du Spectacle Africain
description: Africa's leading performing arts market and festival held biennially in Abidjan. Features theatre, music, dance, and circus from across the continent with professional networking for African performing artists.

name: Fête du Vodoun — Togo
description: Togo's national celebration of Vodoun with elaborate rituals, sacred dances, animal sacrifices, communal ceremonies, and masquerade performances. An important spiritual observance for millions of West Africans.

name: Splashy Fen Music Festival
description: South Africa's longest-running music festival set in the spectacular Drakensberg mountains. Features diverse genres across multiple stages with camping, nature walks, and family-friendly atmosphere.

name: Luxor African Film Festival
description: Annual film festival exclusively dedicated to African cinema, held against the backdrop of Luxor's ancient temples. Showcases films from across 54 African nations promoting continental storytelling.

name: Blankets & Wine
description: Nairobi's beloved monthly outdoor music concert bringing together emerging and established African artists. Fans spread blankets on grass, enjoy wine and food, and experience live Afropop, soul, and jazz.

name: Kigali International Peace Marathon
description: One of Africa's most scenic and meaningful marathon events held on Genocide Memorial Day. Thousands of local and international runners participate through Kigali's famous hills promoting peace, reconciliation, and health.

name: Cape Town Carnival
description: Free-entry annual street carnival along Cape Town's Fan Walk. Features elaborate floats, professional performers, headline musical acts, dance troupes, and illuminated night parade celebrating diversity.

name: Enkutatash (Ethiopian New Year)
description: Ethiopian New Year celebration marking the end of the rainy season. Features colourful flower bunches given as gifts, traditional songs, feasts, national dancing, and church ceremonies across the country.

name: Dakar Fashion Week
description: Africa's most celebrated fashion week spotlighting African designers, traditional textiles, kente, ankara, boubou, and contemporary African fashion to a global audience in Senegal's vibrant capital.

name: Lesotho Sky Basotho Cultural Festival
description: Celebrates Basotho heritage through traditional music, mokorotlo hat ceremonies, horse riding displays, traditional blanket culture, and highland food in the Mountain Kingdom of Africa.

name: Sakifo Musik Festival
description: Réunion Island's biggest music festival blending maloya (traditional Creole music), reggae, world music, and international acts in the island's stunning volcanic scenery.

name: Gabon National Festival of Arts and Culture
description: Annual national independence and culture celebration featuring Gabonese traditional music, Bwiti ceremonial dance, masquerades, forest culture, and ethnic group performances across Libreville.

name: Sauti Festival — Dar es Salaam
description: Tanzania's urban music and arts festival celebrating bongo flava, Afrobeats, and East African contemporary sounds alongside visual art, fashion, and creative industries in the nation's commercial hub.

name: Kano Argungu International Fishing Festival
description: One of Nigeria's most spectacular traditional events where thousands of fishermen simultaneously enter the Matan Fada River for the grand fishing competition. Also features canoe racing, wild duck hunting, and traditional music.

name: Bamako Photo Biennale
description: Africa's most prestigious photography festival dedicated to African photographers. Transforms Bamako into a city-wide gallery of powerful visual storytelling about African lives, landscapes, and cultures.

name: Detty December — Accra
description: Month-long festive season when millions of African diaspora return home to Ghana for nonstop concerts, parties, and festivals. Features major Afrobeats artists, beach parties, jollof cook-offs, and cultural celebrations.

name: Flytime Fest — Lagos
description: Lagos's premier year-end music concert and festival featuring the biggest Afrobeats and Afropop headliners. One of the marquee events of Nigeria's festive season drawing 30,000+ attendees.

name: Mauritius International Creole Festival
description: Celebrates the rich Creole heritage of Mauritius and other Indian Ocean islands through sega music, traditional dance, artisan crafts, Creole cuisine, and colourful cultural performances.

name: Namibia Annual Trade Fair & Cultural Festival
description: Namibia's largest annual gathering bringing together diverse ethnic groups — San, Herero, Himba, Ovambo, and Nama — for cultural displays, traditional attire, business, music, and national heritage showcasing.

name: Africa Month Concerts — Johannesburg
description: Month-long government-backed celebration of African identity and arts held annually in May across Johannesburg's parks, theatres, and cultural centres. Features free outdoor concerts, cultural exhibitions, and heritage programmes.

name: Mwakakogwa Festival — Zanzibar
description: Traditional Zanzibar New Year festival of Persian origin practised in southern Unguja. Marked by ritual sacrifices, traditional dances, symbolic field fighting, and draws participants from across East Africa.

name: SIAO — International Arts & Crafts Fair of Ouagadougou
description: Africa's largest artisan and craft fair held biennially with craftspeople from 35+ African countries displaying textiles, leather goods, sculptures, jewellery, and traditional art alongside live music and dance.

name: Incwala Ceremony — Eswatini
description: Eswatini's most sacred royal ceremony of kingship renewal. Only the King can authorize it. Features warriors gathering sacred plants, national dances, cattle sacrifice, and the tasting of first fruits in a deeply spiritual atmosphere.

name: Irreecha (Oromo Thanksgiving Festival)
description: The largest gathering of the Oromo people — Ethiopia's biggest ethnic group — at Bishoftu Lake to give thanks to Waaqa (God) after the rainy season. Features millions of worshippers in colourful traditional dress, singing, and offerings.

name: Lamu Maulid Festival
description: One of East Africa's most important Islamic celebrations marking the Prophet Muhammad's birthday in the historic Swahili town of Lamu. Features dhow races, devotional music, religious processions, and Swahili poetry recitals.

name: Lagos Jazz Series
description: Nigeria's premier jazz festival celebrating Afro-jazz, highlife, and jazz fusion in a city known for its vibrant music scene. Features leading Nigerian and international jazz artists across elegant Lagos venues.

name: Stellenbosch Wine Festival
description: South Africa's premier wine event in the Cape Winelands featuring tastings from over 100 estates, gourmet food pairings, live music, art, and a celebration of the country's world-class wine heritage.

name: Genna — Ethiopian Christmas
description: Ethiopian Orthodox Christmas celebrated on January 7th. In Lalibela, thousands of white-robed pilgrims gather at the rock-hewn churches for all-night chanting, drumming, and ceremonial processions in the most sacred site in Ethiopia.

name: Carthage International Festival
description: One of North Africa's most prestigious arts festivals held in the ancient Roman theatre of Carthage. Features world-class music, theatre, dance, and opera performances under the stars in a UNESCO World Heritage setting.

name: Sierra Leone Cultural Festival
description: National independence day cultural celebration featuring traditional Temne, Mende, Krio, and Fula cultural performances, mask dances, drumming, and colourful street parades through the capital.

name: Kuomboka Ceremony — Zambia
description: One of Africa's most spectacular royal ceremonies. The Litunga (King) of the Lozi people moves his royal palace from flooded plains to higher ground by royal barge. Features drumming, chanting, and thousands of canoes.

name: Zimbabwe International Trade Fair Cultural Gala
description: Zimbabwe's largest trade and cultural event combining business with traditional Ndebele and Shona performances, mbira music, dance competitions, national cuisine, and heritage exhibitions.

name: Ghana Jollof Food & Music Festival
description: A beloved celebration of Ghanaian cuisine anchored around the legendary jollof rice rivalry with Nigeria. Features celebrity chef cook-offs, street food markets, live highlife and afrobeats music, and cultural pride.

name: Oppikoppi Music Festival
description: South Africa's legendary bush music festival held in the Limpopo bushveld. Features rock, metal, indie, and alternative acts across multiple stages with camping under the stars in a famously dusty and passionate atmosphere.

name: Marrakech International Film Festival
description: One of the most glamorous film festivals in Africa and the Arab world. International and African films compete in the historic medina with celebrity appearances and red carpet events against Marrakech's stunning backdrop.

name: Abuja International Arts & Craft Festival
description: Nigeria's capital hosts artisans from all 36 states and West Africa showcasing hand-crafted textiles, pottery, beadwork, leather goods, and wood carvings alongside live cultural performances and food.

name: Koroga Festival — Nairobi
description: Nairobi's favourite multi-genre outdoor music festival bringing together Afropop, reggae, jazz, and soul acts in a picnic-style setting. A beloved social and cultural weekend event in the Kenyan capital.

name: Burundi Ngoma Festival
description: Celebrates Burundi's world-famous royal drummers (Tambourinaires), a UNESCO Intangible Heritage. Performers in leopard-skin wraps play towering drums in perfectly synchronised formations of extraordinary power and athleticism.

name: Namibia International Music Festival
description: Brings together classical, jazz, choral, and traditional African music artists from Namibia and southern Africa. Celebrates the country's multicultural heritage through music in the striking setting of the Namibian capital.

name: Bamako Marathon & Cultural Week
description: Combines an international marathon through Bamako's streets with a cultural week featuring Malian griot music, kora performances, bogolan textile art demonstrations, and traditional wrestling.
